sourceMovie.title: I Am Legend
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: I Am Legend works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Contagion
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Contagion (2011) clearly understood what makes drama work — the same quality that keeps I Am Legend rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Blindness
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Blindness (2008) is the kind of film I Am Legend fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The drama overlap is real, but it's the tense emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: These Final Hours
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: These Final Hours shares I Am Legend's gift for drama — the 2014 film delivers science-fiction without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: The Omega Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes I Am Legend compelling — the drama, the science-fiction — shows up in The Omega Man (1971) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that tense storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: World War Z
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The science-fiction energy of I Am Legend is alive in World War Z (2013), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a dark payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: The Terminator
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Terminator (1984) operates in the same science-fiction as I Am Legend, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The tense payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: The Matrix Reloaded
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Matrix Reloaded (2003) belongs to the same conversation as I Am Legend — both anchor their science-fiction in thriller emotion that doesn't let go. The tense undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If I Am Legend's science-fiction stayed with you, Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials (2015) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The thriller through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: The Matrix Revolutions
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where I Am Legend excels in science-fiction, The Matrix Revolutions (2003) doubles down on thriller to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: War of the Worlds
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: War of the Worlds (2005) is the natural next step after I Am Legend: same science-fiction DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and thriller elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

faq.question: What should I watch after I Am Legend?
faq.answer: Try Contagion, Blindness, These Final Hours—each shares DNA with I Am Legend while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is I Am Legend?
faq.answer: I Am Legend (2007) is best described as Drama, Science Fiction, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is I Am Legend part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "I Am Legend Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
